section_title: ESSENTIALS TO TRADING THE DAILY BIAS INTRODUCTION
content:
Welcome back. This teaching is specifically dealing with the essentials to trading the daily bias. We're
looking at the dollar index, a daily chart. I want to cover some essentials and my understanding about how
to trade daily bias. This is not to teach you every single day, Monday through Friday, of every month what
the daily bias is going to be tomorrow. I don't have that understanding for every single pair.

section_title: GENERAL APPROACH TO DAILY BIAS FOR SCALPERS
content:
I do know how to find a bias across all the pairs so as a scalper I can excel by looking at using some of
these concepts and obviously things that I teach in my tutorials both free and in my paid mentorship. I'm
going to cover one or two things here but I think are paramount that will help you at least try to get a hold
on daily bias.

section_title: ASCERTAINING DAILY CLOSE AND RISK DISCLOSURE
content:
If I look at a daily chart, what we're trying to do is ascertain the likelihood of it being an up close or a down
close day. There's absolutely no guarantee of what I'm going to tell you here being accurate, so you always
have to use that as a reminder because I'm human and you're human too, so you're going to make
mistakes just as well as I can make a mistake and I can be wrong. I don't want you to take what I'm stating
here as the panacea, be-all end-all to understanding what daily bias is.

section_title: BUILDING DAILY BIAS OPINION AND PRACTICE
content:
There's a number of things that we can do to get a better opinion about what the daily bias will be
tomorrow or today. But it doesn't guarantee results that are going to be positive, so you always have to
consider that. But it's certainly some things that you can practice in a demo account and build your
understanding about daily bias in the sandbox if you will of a demo.

section_title: IDENTIFYING INTERMEDIATE AND SHORT-TERM SWING POINTS
content:
I like to look for intermediate term and short term highs and intermediate term or short term lows. What I
have here is I have this high deleted and I have this low, these highs up here, this low, this low, this high
and this group of lows down here, which is just an extension of this low back here.

section_title: MARKET BEHAVIOR IN NON-TRENDING MODELS
content:
I teach that when the market is not in a trending model and/or a parabolic move up or down, the market's
going to work off of traditional support resistance theory. Now the problem is is which support resistance do
we use? So I look for the key turning points, the real obvious ones. You can use these short-term ones in
here, but the main one is here because the market moved from that low all the way up to this high,
consolidated, tried to go higher, failed, went lower, constant support here, found resistance, found support,
tried one more time, failure, the market moved higher.

section_title: OVERBOUGHT, OVERSOLD, EQUILIBRIUM
content:
So we're looking at the ebb and flow of how the market moves from a point of overbought to oversold to
equilibrium and consolidation and a news move that would take us to an overbought or oversold condition.
That's a range bound market which is where we're seeing the dollar trading right now. It has a slightly
bullish bias to it and has had that for a couple weeks now.

section_title: INITIAL INTERPRETATION FOR DAILY BIAS DIRECTION
content:
For now, I just want to just cover how I interpret the first thing. I look for is are we coming off of a run above
an old high that may see it trade back below it, or are we trading below an old low that may see it trade
back above it? What I do is I look for these intermediate term price points and swing highs and swing lows
on the daily just to see if we have moved above an old high, because we could very easily reject that and
trade lower. Or if we trade below an old low, we could see it come back above it and stay inside the
previous determined range.

section_title: RUNNING ABOVE OLD HIGHS
content:
If we look for candles or daily ranges that run above an old high, chances are we could see it continue
going higher. But we also have to be mindful that we could see an eventual turnaround or reversal and it
could start to go back below that old high, because this could be very easily seen as a false break.

section_title: RUNNING BELOW OLD LOWS
content:
Same way with the old lows as we see here. The market does in fact trade through it, tries to make a lower
low, couldn't do it, failed again, and then started to trade higher. So we have to have these intermediate
term swing points on our daily chart because they're going to be potential reversal points failing to follow
through after breaking an old high or breaking resistance, or failing to make a lower low and trend lower
after breaking below old support.

section_title: APPLYING CLASSIC SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE
content:
When the market's not trending, this is the scenario that it will typically trade within, which is a classic
support resistance theory. The problem with support resistance theory is which high and which low, and
which resistance and which support do you use? So I start with these swing points like this or turning
points where it could potentially be reversals.

section_title: OBSERVING PRICE ACTION AT KEY TURNING POINTS
content:
You can see how price eventually does trade above this high here, finds a measure of resistance, it
consolidates, so we're in a point of equilibrium and the market's digesting whether or not you want to stay
at these levels or trade higher or trade lower. As we see the market trades below these lows in here, can't
rally above and collapses, trades below it, reprices all the way down below an old low, and then rejects,
comes back into an area of consolidation and rejects again. Expansion lower finds a low, cannot find
momentum through it, consolidates around it, tries one more time to go lower and trades higher.

section_title: IDENTIFYING BULLISH MOMENTUM AND RESISTANCE TARGETS
content:
Every time the market breaks a swing high, and if we look to the left of that swing high, where's the next old
intermediate-term high? This one here. So there's nothing for it to potentially reverse inside of until we get
up to here. So if we're going to use simple classic resistance and support levels, we could see that this is
the one we're going to be looking for. So if we're trading around here and we're looking here, this is going
to be the form of resistance, so the market's wanting to try to get back up to that level.

section_title: CONFIRMING BULLISH BIAS THROUGH PRICE ACTION
content:
The market does in fact try to make an attempt to do that, but when we have these ideas suggesting
classic support, short-term resistance, that's what this whole level is here. The market's tried to go through
it one more time, tried to get through an old low, and then traded through it back above this swing high
here. What I'm going to teach you is this is what I look for for daily bias. There's a lot of folks on social
media that were looking for a weaker dollar and ignoring these types of phenomenon.

section_title: DERIVING DAILY BIAS FROM SUPPORT BREAKS AND SWING HIGHS
content:
I'm going to show you here you're going to have that error as well, but if we know that there is a support
level here, we tried several times to go below it, and then we had a swing high broken, what's the market
really telling you? It wants to go higher. So what's the next level to reach for? This short term resistance
level. So we can see that it wants to get to 90.417. That's the high of this candle here.

section_title: BULLISH MARKET FOCUS
content:
What do we do at that point? Once we clear this high here, what's the bias? If you look at this little diagram
I have over here, this is going to refer to the previous day. Now this could be a bullish or bearish day, it
doesn't make a difference. But when the market is bullish, as we've outlined it over here, we're going to be
looking for the previous day's high to be the target and the previous day's low to be supported. In other
words, there shouldn't be any movement below the previous day's low. It should all focus on the buy side,
that means reaching for the previous day's high.

section_title: BEARISH MARKET FOCUS
content:
When the market's bearish, this scenario would be reversed. We would be looking for price having an
inability to get above the previous day's high and seek the previous day's low.

section_title: BULLISH DAILY BIAS EXAMPLE
content:
Using this criteria here, once we broke this candle's swing high here, the daily bias on this candle and this
candle, and this is a Sunday, we would look for Monday as well, and then Tuesday we would look for
bullishness. So if we use this same theory, we're going to draw this particular candle right here is the one
that broke the swing high, and then we know we have a resistance level it could probably reach for. So
we're going to be looking for the next day to trade not to the previous day's low, but to and through previous
day's high. So this next day, what's the daily bias? Here's the opening, what's the bias? It's going to want to
reach for this high, so where is it wanting to go? Up. So what are we going to focus on? Previous day's low
or previous day's high? Previous day's high, because it's bullish, it just wanted to go higher. So the market
trades up, creates up close day.

section_title: CONTINUED BULLISH BIAS APPLICATION
content:
Then we take this same thing and apply it to this candle. We have a very simple approach there's no box in
the hood up here now. We have yesterday, this is what this one, this candle's date on December 8th. This
candle's last Thursday's high, which would be 9380. Market opens, we're going to see it trade through that
and with the anticipation it's going to eventually try to reach for this daily high.

section_title: PRIORITIZING HIGH TARGETS IN BULLISH CONTEXT
content:
When this happens again, we don't think about the daily low from the previous day. We're focusing on the
movement through the previous day's high and/or eventually reaching to this level here on the daily. The
market closes in the middle of the range here is Sunday's trading. Then Monday the same phenomenon,
you would look for the market to want to trade through what on Monday? We would look for this previous
Friday's low to be supported and we would look for a price to run through previous Friday's high.

section_title: ANALYSIS OF MONDAY'S PRICE ACTION AND BIAS
content:
Again, this is Sunday, we wouldn't expect anything there. Monday we see it open here, a little bit of
movement above it, but it does in fact pierce Friday's low and it comes back and closes where it opens. So
is there any significant change in the underlying direction? No. Did we trade to this high yet? No.

section_title: CURRENT WEEK DAILY BIAS ANALYSIS (DEC 2017)
content:
So we take that same premise now we apply it to this candle which is Monday of this particular week of the
recording. And we see it open here so what's the bias? We're looking for the previous day's high, why?
Because we have yet to trade back to this old resistance level. Market opens here, trades up, trades
through it, but look what it does, it comes right back inside the daily range.

section_title: INTRADAY CONSIDERATIONS FOR DAILY BIAS
content:
In the next trading session, right now at the time of this recording it's 9:26 p.m. New York time on December
12, 2017, and the candle is already starting here. It opened, had a little bit of movement up and now we're
moving a little bit lower. So it may want to trade a little bit higher, go a little bit higher through this old high.
But as we're reaching for these intermediate term levels on the daily chart, we're factoring in does it have
enough range to reach for it above or below?

section_title: CONFIRMING DAILY BIAS WITH PREVIOUS DAY'S RANGE
content:
Then we take that approach and apply it immediately to the previous day's high and low as we have here.
What you're determining is if we're bullish, we're going to be focusing on the previous day's high for
momentum and if we're bearish, we focus on the previous day's low for momentum.

section_title: SIGNALS OF INDECISION AND REVERSAL
content:
Very rarely do you see both sides of the previous day's candle tagged or traded through. If it does, it's
usually indecisiveness or it's going to be a reversal soon, and they're clearing the board on both sides of
the marketplace. When you start seeing those types of things, get prepared because the market could be
in fact about to reverse. You can see that actually happening here: previous day's high and low here, the
next day it trades through it and its low is violated too. So we're about to see a reversal next day, reversal.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING THE OUTSIDE DAY PATTERN
content:
I'll show you another example of that. See here we have it here as well. We have a Thursday's range. Now
this low comes in at 94.42 and a low in here is 94.42 so it's equal but still very, very close to the high. This
is Sunday and a Monday. Nothing shown there, but end up getting a very important high eventually
afterwards. And now this is called an outside day when you have a previous day's range that's violated.

section_title: OUTSIDE DAY WITH A DOWN CLOSE (BULLISH SIGNAL)
content:
This is an example to this high is 93.98 and the low is 93.67. This candle's low is 93.58 and the high is
90.99. So this is an outside day with a down close. That's typically bullish. Reason why, it creates a false
break above the previous day's high and then closes it on the low. If we're in a bullish market overall and
that occurs, generally sets up a really good buy scenario. I've learned that from Larry Williams, and that's
from like old school stuff.

section_title: LARRY WILLIAMS' INTERPRETATION OF OUTSIDE DAY
content:
But you got that context behind it. Can't just take an outside day with a down close and this naturally this
film is going to be a bullish today. But you can see it does in fact give a huge opportunity to be a buyer on
this day. My application of outside day is when it does that, it sets up a potential short-term reversal. What
Larry Williams brought to the table with it is it creates a short-term oversold scenario, so it doesn't
necessarily mean it's a reversal, just means it's many times a continuation pattern according to his
definitions.

section_title: ANOTHER OUTSIDE DAY EXAMPLE AND REVERSAL
content:
Here's another scenario here, outside day. We have the candle here, the high comes in at 93.23 and the
low is 92.81. The very next day the high is taken out 93.24, the low is 92.75. So reversal day. Then we can
see the market does in fact trade higher as a result. So you will always get the outside day at turning
points, but I'd like to look for it because it builds a lot of confidence behind it and by using these tools.

section_title: CONFIRMING OVERALL BULLISHNESS BY SWING BREAKS
content:
When the market also breaks more or less a swing high of importance and we have a level that doesn't
have anything else until we get to here, it's bullish. It's bullish. Avoid this whole scenario like looking at this
high to this low point, feds don't looking for optimal trade entry, you didn't sell off a lot stuff. It's all patience
and willingness to want to go below this low multiple times and then it broke a swing high. So the
momentum is bullish and that's going to reach for this.

section_title: DOLLAR INDEX AND FOREIGN CURRENCY DAILY BIAS
content:
If we're doing that analysis on a daily for the dollar index and we've arrived at a bullishness for the dollar,
what does that translate for foreign currency in terms of daily bias? It's going to be a mirror image. So if we
go to the Euro Dollar, we can see that the Euro Dollar has been bearish as we would reasonably expect.

section_title: EURO DOLLAR BEARISH DAILY BIAS APPLICATION
content:
Notice that every single day the daily high is not concerned with at all, it's the previous day's low it trades
through it. So now we have a low here, what's it going to do? It's going to want to trade to the previous
day's low, it trades through it. So what's the next story line? Opens, trades down below the previous day's
low. Next day, what's it going to do? It's going to trade through not only the previous day's low but what's it
really reaching for? That old intermediate term low on the daily. I'll give you some lipstick on the charts, you
can see that framing out a little bit.

section_title: EURO DOLLAR AS A MIRROR IMAGE OF THE DOLLAR INDEX
content:
So this low here and its price is starting to drop down it's reaching for this level here. Why? Because it's
basically the opposite of what we're seeing. The dollar is reaching for this intermediate term high, while the
Euro Dollar is reaching for an intermediate term low. Each day we're focusing on again this phenomenon
here. We're focusing on for the Euro because the dollar is bullish, we're focusing on the previous day's low
and that's where it's going to be aiming for, to it and through it. That's how you think about it. Mind's bearish.

section_title: IDENTIFYING BEARISH PATTERNS IN EURO DOLLAR
content:
Back to the Euro Dollar, every single trading day opens, creates a small little tail, Power of Three, Judas
swing, sell it down, close, reach for the previous day's low. Next day opens, false rally, Judas swing, sell it,
reach for the previous day's low, does it. Next day opens, rallies, false rally, Judas swing, sell it, fade it,
reach for the previous day's low, boom, handled.

section_title: SUSTAINED BEARISH OUTLOOK FOR EURO DOLLAR
content:
Next day open, small little tail, trades lower, comes back off of the low, okay, but we're still targeting here.
We're still reaching for that. Next day, well this is going to be Sunday so here's a Monday trading range. It
opens, rallies up because the short term overbought scenario comes off the highs. That is a potential
reversal scenario. The next day same, same scenario. We're looking for what? The market to open, trade
below the previous day's low and then ultimately if it trades through that, forget Sunday, go to Friday, it
trades through Friday's range and then ultimately reach for this low here. So we're looking for that scenario
here potentially to trade through this low still is there.

section_title: CURRENT DAILY BIAS FOR EURO DOLLAR (DEC 12, 2017)
content:
So the daily bias today on December 12, 2017 should be a move lower below this low using what I'm
teaching you here.

section_title: CONCLUSION AND FURTHER RESOURCES
content:
Hopefully this has been insightful to you guys. It gives you a little bit more framework to work with in.
Obviously there's so many things that we can build on to build bias and inter market relationships as well.
But I try to incorporate the application of the dollar index analysis and also to justify what we would view in
terms of foreign currencies bullish or bearish news as well and using daily bias. Okay folks, hopefully you
enjoyed this presentation. If you like this presentation you want to find more, you can visit my website at B
inner circle trader.com.
